en: to make new tears, and special tubes called lacrimal puncta to drain old ones away.
ko: 오래된 눈물을 배출하는 눈물점이라는 특별한 관이 있어요.

en: And she cries so much that she goes through ten ounces of tears per day,
ko: 그리고 그녀는 하루에 10온스의 눈물을 흘릴 정도로 많이 울어요.

en: 30 gallons a year. In fact, if you look closely,
you'll see that she's crying a little bit all the time.
ko: 그녀가 항상 조금씩 울고 있다는 걸 알 수 있어요.

en: The basal tears that Iris constantly produces form a thin coating of three layers
that cover her and keep dirt and debris away.
Right next to Iris is the mucus layer, which keeps the whole thing fastened to her.
On top of it is the aqueous layer, which keeps Iris hydrated, repels invasive bacteria,
and protects her skin, or cornea, from damage.
ko: 그녀의 피부, 즉 각막을 손상으로부터 보호해줘요.

en: And finally, there is the lipid layer,
an oily outer film that keeps the surface smooth for Iris to see through,
ko: 아이리스가 잘 볼 수 있도록 표면을 매끄럽게 유지하는 기름진 바깥막으로,

en: and prevents the other layers from evaporating.
Normally, Iris goes about her day without really noticing the basal tears doing their thing.
That's kind of their whole point. But one day, she meets a girl named Onion.
ko: 그게 바로 기초 눈물의 존재 이유예요. 그런데 어느 날...
